name: Calibration Data
oneliner: A small dataset (~128 samples) run through the model to collect activation statistics needed by quantization methods to set scale factors.
explanation: Calibration data is a small representative dataset used to measure how activations behave inside a model, so quantization methods can set accurate scaling parameters. The standard benchmark established by the GPTQ paper is 128 samples of 2048 tokens from the C4 or WikiText-2 datasets. Methods like GPTQ, AWQ, and SmoothQuant require calibration because they need to observe real activation ranges, while simpler approaches like round-to-nearest and dynamic quantization do not. Using domain-matched calibration data yields slightly better results.
fundamentals: For layer Y=XW+b: calibration records per-layer activation stats. Approaches: min/max (simple but outlier-sensitive), percentile (clips outliers), MSE-optimal (minimizes reconstruction error), entropy/KL-based (TensorRT). For GPTQ: calibration computes Hessian H = 2XX^T, used to solve layer-wise optimization min_Q $\|\|WX - QX\|\|^2$.

resources.label: White Paper on Neural Network Quantization
